misconceptionSummary: Students compare decimal digits as if they are integers, ignoring column alignment.

steps.narration: The student stripped the decimal point and compared 09 with 9 as if they were whole numbers. But 09 and 9 are not in the same columns — one is hundredths, the other is tenths.

visuals.ruleText: Always compare digit by digit in the same column — never strip the decimal point
visuals.subText: Pad the shorter decimal so both have the same number of places

steps.narration: Decimal digits can only be compared when they sit in the same column. Pad the shorter number with trailing zeros so every digit has a matching partner.
After padding, compare left to right. The ones digits tie, so move to tenths. Zero tenths is less than nine tenths — the comparison is settled right there.

visuals.label: Pad → compare column by column → tenths decide

steps.narration: Three point zero nine is less than three point nine. Padding with a zero reveals the truth — never compare decimal tails as if they are integers.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: treating 3.09 and 3.9 as if the parts after the decimal are integers (09 vs 9). Align columns first — 3.09 vs 3.90 shows 3.09 is smaller.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: You cannot compare decimal digits without column alignment. Pad both decimals to the same length, then compare place by place from the left.
